task_completion.description: The abdominal CT scan is successfully loaded into the medical image viewer (e.g., 3D Slicer).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application interface is visible with an abdominal CT scan loaded, typically showing axial, sagittal, and coronal views.

task_completion.description: The view is navigated to the porta hepatis region to locate the portal triad.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial view is centered on the liver hilum. The portal vein (large, circular/oval structure) is visible. The agent may be adjusting window/level settings to visualize soft tissue.

task_completion.description: A measurement tool is used to measure the diameter of the Common Bile Duct (CBD).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler or length measurement annotation is drawn across a small, round, low-attenuation structure located anterior and lateral to the portal vein. A numerical length value (in mm) is visible next to the annotation.

task_completion.description: The agent generates a JSON report containing the diameter, anatomical level, and classification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the content of 'cbd_report.json'. It includes fields for 'diameter_mm', 'anatomical_level', and 'classification'. The classification logic (Normal/Borderline/Dilated) is consistent with the measured diameter.

task_completion.description: The measurement markup and report files are saved to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer or save dialog shows 'cbd_measurement.mrk.json' and 'cbd_report.json' located in '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/'.

integrity.description: The measurement was performed using the software's GUI tools, not via programmatic injection without visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the measurement toolbar or the measurement line appearing on the image canvas.

integrity.description: The value recorded in the report matches the measurement shown on the screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'diameter_mm' value in the JSON report text matches the measurement value displayed on the CT scan annotation.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the medical imaging software directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not simply writing a file from a terminal based on pre-existing knowledge; the medical viewer is the active window during the measurement phase.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Common Bile Duct (CBD) is located anterior and lateral to the portal vein. Normal diameter is ≤ 6.0 mm. Borderline is 7.0-8.0 mm. Dilated is > 8.0 mm. Use these thresholds to verify the correctness of the 'classification' field in the report based on the visible measurement.
